scenarios.task: Our A/B test of the new checkout flow finished: treatment shows +2.8% orders per user, p=0.003, n=400k per arm over 14 days. PM wants to ship tomorrow. The assignment split was supposed to be 50/50 but the dashboard shows 398,104 vs 411,882 users. Green light?
criteria: Runs or demands an SRM (sample ratio mismatch) check on the 398k vs 412k split and treats a failure as invalidating the readout regardless of the p-value
Explains the imbalance is far beyond chance for a 50/50 split (a chi-square would flag it), implying broken assignment or logging
Refuses to certify the +2.8% until the assignment mechanism is diagnosed, rather than shipping on the significant p-value
Names concrete causes to investigate (bot filtering asymmetry, redirect drops, logging failures)
Also asks about guardrail metrics and/or novelty effects rather than only the primary metric

scenarios.task: Weekly active users dropped 8% this week and leadership is panicking. The head of growth already has a deck blaming the new pricing page. I have 4 hours. What's my triage order?
criteria: Puts instrumentation/tracking breakage first in the triage order, before any behavioral explanation
Checks metric definition changes and data pipeline/ETL issues before accepting a real user-behavior drop
Checks mix effects and seasonality/calendar effects (holiday, weekday composition) before a causal story
Treats the pre-existing pricing-page narrative as a hypothesis to test, and proposes a segmentation check (did the drop concentrate in users who saw the page?)
Only reaches 'real product effect' after cheaper explanations are excluded

scenarios.task: Marketing wants to know the revenue impact of last quarter's brand campaign. It ran nationally, so there's no holdout. They point out revenue rose 15% in the campaign quarter vs prior quarter. Give me an honest analysis plan.
criteria: Rejects the naive pre/post 15% comparison as confounded by seasonality, trend, and concurrent changes
Proposes a specific quasi-experimental design (diff-in-diff against a comparison series, synthetic control, or similar) and explains what identification requires
States the failure modes/assumptions of the chosen method (parallel trends, contamination) instead of presenting it as clean truth
Recommends designing future campaigns with geo holdouts or staggered rollout for real measurement
Is honest that the retroactive estimate will carry wide uncertainty, and quantifies rather than hides it
